story.iWant: to implement message history compression using sliding window và summarization
story.soThat: I can reduce prompt token count for long conversations while preserving critical context và quality

subtasks.subtask: Integrate với existing LLM calls for summarization
Test MessageHistoryCompressor class với sample conversations
Define sliding window size (e.g., keep last 10 messages)
Implement logic to keep recent messages unchanged
Test sliding window với different conversation lengths
Use LLM to summarize older messages
Test conversation flow với compressed history
Track token count before và after compression

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Sliding window được implemented để keep recent messages
Older messages được summarized using LLM với quality checks
Context preservation được tested through conversation flow
Token reduction for message history được measured

doc.title: ChainLens AI Worker - Epic Breakdown: LLM Optimization
doc.snippet: Story 2.2 defines requirements for implementing message history compression. Expected savings: $5-10/month with minimal quality impact (<5%). Effort: 3 hours. Prerequisites: Story 1.4.

doc.title: ChainLens AI Worker - Master Optimization Plan v1.1
doc.section: Phase 2: Quality-Preserving Medium Optimizations - Task 2.2: Message History Compression
doc.snippet: Message history compression uses sliding window to keep recent messages and LLM summarization for older messages. Quality checks on summaries required. Context preservation must be tested. Token reduction measured and tracked.

doc.title: Research Prompt: System Prompt Optimization
doc.snippet: Message history compression techniques: sliding window (keep recent N messages), summarization (LLM-based), and context preservation. Quality checks required. Token reduction: 10-20% for long conversations.

file.reason: New file to create. Implement MessageHistoryCompressor class với sliding window logic, LLM summarization, and quality checks.
Primary method to modify for integrating message history compression. Need to compress message history before building system prompt.
LLM API call handler. Used for summarization of older messages. Can use existing LLM infrastructure for summarization.
Configuration management. Can add compression configuration (sliding window size, summarization settings) here.

constraint.description: Minimal quality impact (<5%) acceptable. Quality maintained at 95-100% (monitored). Context preservation must be tested through conversation flow.
Dual-mode architecture: Original mode (baseline) + Optimized mode (with compression). Feature flags allow switching and rollback. Quality monitoring required.
Sliding window size: Configurable (default: last 10 messages). Recent messages kept unchanged. Older messages summarized using LLM.
LLM summarization for older messages. Quality checks on summaries required. Can use same LLM or dedicated summarization model. Summarization adds latency but reduces tokens.
Critical context must be preserved through compression. Context preservation must be tested through conversation flow. Test with different conversation types.
Must not break existing LLM calls. Compression should be transparent to callers. Test with existing agent configurations.

interface.name: MessageHistoryCompressor class
interface.signature: class MessageHistoryCompressor: def __init__(self, sliding_window_size: int = 10): ... async def compress_history(self, messages: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]: ...

interface.name: PromptManager.build_system_prompt()
interface.signature: async def build_system_prompt(model_name: str, agent_config: Optional[dict], thread_id: str, mcp_wrapper_instance: Optional[MCPToolWrapper], client=None, tool_registry=None, xml_tool_calling: bool = True) -> dict

interface.name: make_llm_api_call()
interface.signature: async def make_llm_api_call(messages: List[Dict[str, Any]], model_name: str, ...) -> Union[Dict[str, Any], AsyncGenerator, ModelResponse]

tests.standards: Testing standards include: Quality validation (95-100% similarity check for compressed vs non-compressed), Performance testing (token reduction, cost savings, latency impact), Integration testing (existing LLM calls, different models, agent configurations, long conversations, context preservation). Use semantic similarity checking for quality validation. A/B testing framework for comparing original vs optimized modes.

test.idea: Unit test: Verify MessageHistoryCompressor class creation và initialization
Integration test: Test message history compression với LLM calls
Unit test: Verify sliding window logic (keep last N messages unchanged)
Integration test: Test sliding window với different conversation lengths
Unit test: Verify message summarization logic và quality checks
Integration test: Test summarization quality với sample conversations
Integration test: Test conversation flow với compressed history và verify context preservation
Quality validation test: Test với different conversation types và verify critical context is preserved
Unit test: Verify token counting và reduction measurement
Integration test: Test token reduction với different conversation lengths
Automated test: Compare compressed vs non-compressed responses (target: 95-100% similarity)
A/B test: Run same conversations with original and optimized modes, compare quality metrics
